signals.headline: IUML Candidate Announcements**: The party named 25 candidates across constituencies, fielding two women candidates for t
signals.raw: IUML Candidate Announcements**: The party named 25 candidates across constituencies, fielding two women candidates for the first time in its history—Fathima Thahliya (Perambra) and Jayanthi Rajan (Koothuparambu)[1][3]. Senior leader MK Muneer was excluded reportedly due to health concerns, with PK Firoz replacing him in Koduvalli[1].

signals.raw: Internal Party Tensions**: The IUML's women's wing national general secretary Noorbina Rasheed resigned following the candidate announcement, expressing displeasure over limited Women's League representation[3].
